title: Sirius Red staining of murine tissues
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"fb2hv","text":"Sirius Red staining is a usefull staining to study collagen distribution. In adipose tissue development during obesity the extracellular matrix is being remodeled and this can be analyzed using Sirius Red staining. In addition, fibrosis in livers from mice suffering from non-alcoholic steatohepatitis can be identified using this staining. Moreover, scar formation after the induction of a myocardial infarct can be illustrated using the Sirius Red staining.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"3tthr","text":"To weigh off Picric acid, the entire bottle is weighed before and after some Picric acid is transfered to another recipient.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"gn0a","text":"-Due to its explosive characteristics Picric acid is delivered as a solution (powder moisted with water) and is kept within a locked separate ventilated cabinet.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"fu4nv","text":"-Waste is collected in a separate dedicated waste container.\n-Due to toxicity of xylene, steps 1-5 and steps 10-13 are performed in a fume hood and xylene is handled with xylene-resistant gloves.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: 90 min Sirius Red solution * 8g Picric acid in 200 ml AD (= 4% saturated solution) * Stirr 30 min * Filter * Add 0.2g Direct-Red * Stirr * Filter Picric acid Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #197378 Direct-Red 80 Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #365548
2 min HCl 0.01N (1,015x dilution: 1.97 ml saturated HCl + 2,000 ml AD)
Mount coverslip with DPX DPX Merck Millipore (EMD Millipore) Catalog #1.00579.0500
creator: Bianca Hemmeryckx
